summary: Read all 5 articles in Robinhood's newsroom design category, captured lessons per article, and mapped each lesson to Owlory surfaces with applicability ratings (high/medium/low/not-applicable). Preserved findings under automation/proofs/app-design-research-robinhood-newsroom-lessons/manifest.json. No source/test/UI changes. Recommended 3 optional follow-up slices: integrated content standards reference, error-message audit, design-vision-metaphor ADR — none committed to in this slice.
highest_value_lessons: Article #3 (Integrated Content Standards): integrate content-design guidance into the design system rather than keep it separate. Owlory has DesignSystem + L() + Localizable.strings, but content rules (CTA capitalization, error-message phrasing, header punctuation) live separately.
Article #5 (Robinhood Retirement): plug-and-play semantic types for non-linear discovery paths validate Owlory's existing ContinueSubtitleKind + FocusSuggestionRules.Reason structural refactors.
Article #1 (New Visual Identity): 'less is more' + 'purposeful pops' validate Owlory's existing minimal-Today + brandPrimary-accent direction.
explicit_non_applicables: Fintech-specific framing (broker, retirement product, APY, regulatory disclaimers)
Field research at scale (campus intercepts, lit reviews)
not_claimed: Robinhood's design system is directly portable to Owlory
Any of the queued follow-up recommendations should be implemented (project-owner decides)
All five articles are equally instructive (article #4 'Winding Career Paths' is biographical with minimal design content; rated low/none honestly)
residual_risks: WebFetch returned no canonical hrefs from the JavaScript-rendered index page; URLs were resolved via WebSearch site queries. If a future article is added to the category that doesn't surface in Google's site index, this approach would miss it.
Lessons were extracted via WebFetch's LLM summarization rather than direct reading. The summaries are accurate to the article topics but could miss nuance an in-person reader would catch.
recommended_next_reason: Two slices remain queued (app-history-strip-claude-trailers pri 96 and the recommended follow-ups in this manifest are not yet queued). Project-owner decides whether to queue any of the recommended follow-ups.
